2026-04-22T01:47:18.5095023Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-22T01:47:18.5095526Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-22T01:47:18.5095939Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-22T01:47:18.5096292Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-22T01:47:18.5096584Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T01:47:18.5096809Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T01:47:18.5096980Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-22T01:47:18.5097150Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T01:47:18.5097312Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-22T01:47:18.5097530Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-22T01:47:18.5097718Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T01:47:18.5097984Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-22T01:47:18.5098226Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. The commit added four resource types — `ComputeLimitFeatureResource`, `ComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionResource`, `ComputeLimitSharedLimitResource`, and `ComputeLimitVmFamilyResource` — plus their respective collection and data classes, and a `Disable` operation on `ComputeLimitFeatureResource`.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Key concepts section** is a generic link with no package-specific explanation; it does not describe the resource hierarchy (`Feature → GuestSubscription → SharedLimit → VmFamily`) or the purpose of the service (sharing compute quota limits across subscriptions)\\n- **Examples section** only links to a generic samples page; there are no package-specific code snippets showing how to authenticate, list features, enable/disable a feature, manage guest subscriptions, or manage VM family shared limits\\n- **No sample test files** exist under `sdk/computelimit/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit/tests/Samples/` — the `Update-Snippets.ps1` tooling has no source to pull from\\n- **README does not mention** the four main resource types added in this GA release (`ComputeLimitFeatureResource`, `ComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionResource`, `ComputeLimitSharedLimitResource`, `ComputeLimitVmFamilyResource`) or the `Disable`/`Enable` operations\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section — Edit\\n\\nReplace the **Key concepts** section content with package-specific information:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThe `Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit` library manages the following resource types:\\n\\n| Resource | ARM type | Description |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `ComputeLimitFeatureResource` | `Microsoft.ComputeLimit/locations/features` | A compute limit feature (e.g., shared quota) that can be enabled or disabled per subscription-location |\\n| `ComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionResource` | `Microsoft.ComputeLimit/locations/features/guestSubscriptions` | A guest subscription that has been granted access to a shared compute limit |\\n| `ComputeLimitSharedLimitResource` | `Microsoft.ComputeLimit/locations/features/sharedLimits` | A shared quota limit configuration attached to a feature |\\n| `ComputeLimitVmFamilyResource` | `Microsoft.ComputeLimit/locations/vmFamilies` | A VM family whose quota can be shared across guest subscriptions |\\n\\nThe top-level extension entry point is `SubscriptionResource.GetComputeLimitFeatures(location)` and `SubscriptionResource.GetComputeLimitVmFamilies(location)`.\\n```\\n\\nReplace the **Examples** section with snippet placeholders:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### List features in a subscription-location\\n\\n```\\n```\\n(closing fence here)\\n\\n### Enable a compute limit client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n\\n            AzureLocation location = AzureLocation.EastUS;\\n            string featureName = \\u0026#34;SharedQuota\\u0026#34;;\\n            ComputeLimitFeatureResource feature = await subscription.GetComputeLimitFeatures(location).GetAsync(featureName);\\n\\n            ComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionCollection guestSubscriptions = feature.GetComputeLimitGuestSubscriptions();\\n            string guestSubscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            ComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionData data = new ComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionData();\\n            ArmOperation createOp =\\n                await guestSubscriptions.CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, guestSubscriptionId, data);\\n The commit added four resource types — `ComputeLimitFeatureResource`, `ComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionResource`, `ComputeLimitSharedLimitResource`, and `ComputeLimitVmFamilyResource` — plus their respective collection and data classes, and a `Disable` operation on `ComputeLimitFeatureResource`.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Key concepts section** is a generic link with no package-specific explanation; it does not describe the resource hierarchy (`Feature → GuestSubscription → SharedLimit → VmFamily`) or the purpose of the service (sharing compute quota limits across subscriptions)\\n- **Examples section** only links to a generic samples page; there are no package-specific code snippets showing how to authenticate, list features, enable/disable a feature, manage guest subscriptions, or manage VM family shared limits\\n- **No sample test files** exist under `sdk/computelimit/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit/tests/Samples/` — the `Update-Snippets.ps1` tooling has no source to pull from\\n- **README does not mention** the four main resource types added in this GA release (`ComputeLimitFeatureResource`, `ComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionResource`, `ComputeLimitSharedLimitResource`, `ComputeLimitVmFamilyResource`) or the `Disable`/`Enable` operations\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### `sdk/computelimit/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit/README.md` — Edit\\n\\nReplace the **Key concepts** section content with package-specific information:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThe `Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit` library manages the following resource types:\\n\\n| Resource | ARM type | Description |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `ComputeLimitFeatureResource` | `Microsoft.ComputeLimit/locations/features` | A compute limit feature (e.g., shared quota) that can be enabled or disabled per subscription-location |\\n| `ComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionResource` | `Microsoft.ComputeLimit/locations/features/guestSubscriptions` | A guest subscription that has been granted access to a shared compute limit |\\n| `ComputeLimitSharedLimitResource` | `Microsoft.ComputeLimit/locations/features/sharedLimits` | A shared quota limit configuration attached to a feature |\\n| `ComputeLimitVmFamilyResource` | `Microsoft.ComputeLimit/locations/vmFamilies` | A VM family whose quota can be shared across guest subscriptions |\\n\\nThe top-level extension entry point is `SubscriptionResource.GetComputeLimitFeatures(location)` and `SubscriptionResource.GetComputeLimitVmFamilies(location)`.\\n```\\n\\nReplace the **Examples** section with snippet placeholders:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### List features in a subscription-location\\n\\n```\\n```\\n(closing fence here)\\n\\n### Enable a compute limit feature\\n\\n```\\n```\\n(closing fence here)\\n\\n### Disable a compute limit feature\\n\\n```\\n```\\n(closing fence here)\\n\\n### Add a guest subscription to a feature\\n\\n```\\n```\\n(closing fence here)\\n\\n### List VM families\\n\\n```\\n```\\n(closing fence here)\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate a new test file at client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n\\n            AzureLocation location = AzureLocation.EastUS;\\n            ComputeLimitFeatureCollection features = subscription.GetComputeLimitFeatures(location);\\n\\n            await foreach (ComputeLimitFeatureResource feature in features)\\n            {\\n client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n\\n            AzureLocation location = AzureLocation.EastUS;\\n            string featureName = \\u0026#34;SharedQuota\\u0026#34;;\\n            ComputeLimitFeatureResource feature = await subscription.GetComputeLimitFeatures(location).GetAsync(featureName);\\n\\n            ArmOperation operation = await feature.EnableAsync(WaitUntil.Completed);\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Enable operation status: {operation.Value.Status}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n\\n            AzureLocation location = AzureLocation.EastUS;\\n            string featureName = \\u0026#34;SharedQuota\\u0026#34;;\\n            ComputeLimitFeatureResource feature = await subscription.GetComputeLimitFeatures(location).GetAsync(featureName);\\n\\n            ComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionCollection guestSubscriptions = feature.GetComputeLimitGuestSubscriptions();\\n            string guestSubscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            ComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionData data = new ComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionData();\\n            ArmOperation createOp =\\n                await guestSubscriptions.CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, guestSubscriptionId, data);\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Added guest subscription: {createOp.Value.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying The error mentions a missing resource provider for a recent API version, but the resources were created via an ARM template using `2020-07-01`, while the SDK doesn't expose which version it uses.\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\nThe method call to succeed.\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nWhen deleting PIPs (same for NSGs and NICs) we get the following error message:\\n```\\nNo registered resource provider found for location 'westeurope' and API version '2025-03-01' for type 'publicIPAddresses'. The supported api-versions are '2014-12-01-preview, 2015-05-01-preview, 2015-06-15, 2016-03-30, 2016-06-01, 2016-07-01, 2016-08-01, 2016-09-01, 2016-10-01, 2016-11-01, 2016-12-01, 2017-03-01, 2017-04-01, 2017-06-01, 2017-08-01, 2017-09-01, 2017-10-01, 2017-11-01, 2018-01-01, 2018-02-01, 2018-03-01, 2018-04-01, 2018-05-01, 2018-06-01, 2018-07-01, 2018-08-01, 2018-10-01, 2018-11-01, 2018-12-01, 2019-02-01, 2019-04-01, 2019-06-01, 2019-07-01, 2019-08-01, 2019-09-01, 2019-11-01, 2019-12-01, 2020-01-01, 2020-03-01, 2020-04-01, 2020-05-01, 2020-06-01, 2020-07-01, 2020-08-01, 2020-11-01, 2021-01-01, 2021-02-01, 2021-03-01, 2021-04-01, 2021-05-01, 2021-06-01, 2021-08-01, 2021-12-01, 2022-01-01, 2022-05-01, 2022-07-01, 2022-09-01, 2022-11-01, 2023-02-01, 2023-04-01, 2023-05-01, 2023-06-01, 2023-09-01, 2023-11-01, 2024-01-01, 2024-03-01, 2024-05-01, 2024-07-01, 2024-10-01, 2025-01-01'. The supported locations are 'westus, eastus, northeurope, westeurope, eastasia, southeastasia, northcentralus, southcentralus, centralus, eastus2, japaneast, japanwest, brazilsouth, australiaeast, australiasoutheast, centralindia, southindia, westindia, canadacentral, canadaeast, westcentralus, westus2, ukwest, uksouth, koreacentral, koreasouth, francecentral, australiacentral, southafricanorth, uaenorth, switzerlandnorth, germanywestcentral, norwayeast, westus3, jioindiawest, swedencentral, qatarcentral, polandcentral, italynorth, israelcentral, mexicocentral, spaincentral, newzealandnorth, indonesiacentral, austriaeast, chilecentral, malaysiawest'.\\n```\\n\\n### Reproduction Steps\\n\\n```\\nvar resourceGroupName = \\\"abc\\\";\\nvar resourcesPrefix = \\\"abc\\\";\\n\\nvar subscriptionId = \\\"abc\\\";\\nvar tenantId = \\\"abc\\\";\\nvar clientId = \\\"abc\\\";\\nvar clientSecret = \\\"abc\\\";\\n\\nvar credentials = new ClientSecretCredential(\\n    tenantId,\\n    clientId,\\n    clientSecret);\\nvar armClient = new ArmClient(credentials);\\n\\nstring resourceGroupId = $\\\"/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}\\\";\\nvar rg = armClient.GetResourceGroupResource(ResourceIdentifier.Parse(resourceGroupId))?.Get()?.Value;\\nvar resources = rg?.GetGenericResources($\\\"substringof(\\\\'{resourcesPrefix}\\\\', name)\\\", null, 20).ToList();\\n\\nforeach (var resource in resources)\\n{\\n    try\\n    {\\n        await resource.DeleteAsync(WaitUntil.Started);\\n        Console.WriteLine($\\\"Deleting resource: {resource.Id}\\\");\\n    }\\n    catch (Exception ex)\\n    {\\n        Console.WriteLine($\\\"Error processing resource {resource.Id}: {ex.Message}\\\");\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Environment\\n\\n_No
